I agree with the selected answer D. Here is my brief addition to support this decision:

While the smear layer (Option A) can significantly reduce dentine permeability by sealing tubules, fluid convection (Option B) and the length of dentinal tubules (Option C) play crucial roles in regulating how substances move through dentine. Therefore, the only remaining option that doesn’t inherently influence dentine permeability is the diffusion coefficient (Option D). Although the diffusion coefficient affects transport processes, it is generally considered part of the broader physiological dynamics rather than an independent governing factor distinct from intrinsic tissue properties.

**Answer:** D
